prompt:
Building the resumable-download engine: we track which byte ranges of a file are already on disk, ask what's missing, and hand the missing pieces to fetch workers. The tracking structure is this crate. Tests are in tests/intervals.rs and are the spec — implement src/lib.rs (crate rs-intervalset) until cargo test --offline is green. Don't touch tests/, Cargo.toml, .gitignore. No dependencies.

pub struct IntervalSet — a set of half-open intervals [start, end) over u64, kept NORMALIZED at all times: sorted ascending, non-overlapping, and non-adjacent (spans that touch end-to-start are merged into one; [0,5) then [5,10) is [0,10)), never an empty span. That invariant is checked after every mutation in the tests.

API:
- new() -> IntervalSet (empty; also derive/impl Default)
- insert(&mut self, start: u64, end: u64) — add a range, merging with everything it overlaps OR touches. One insert can weld several existing spans together.
- remove(&mut self, start: u64, end: u64) — subtract a range: trims edges, deletes swallowed spans, and SPLITS a span in two when the removed range is strictly inside it.
- contains(&self, point: u64) -> bool — start inclusive, end exclusive.
- covers(&self, start: u64, end: u64) -> bool — is the whole query range covered? Empty queries (start >= end) are vacuously true, even over a gap or on an empty set.
- gaps(&self, start: u64, end: u64) -> Vec<(u64, u64)> — the uncovered sub-ranges within the window, clipped to it, sorted; whole window when the set is empty; empty vec for an empty window.
- iter(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = (u64, u64)> + '_ — the normalized spans, sorted; borrows (calling it twice works).
- len(&self) -> usize (number of spans), is_empty(&self) -> bool, total(&self) -> u64 (sum of span lengths).

Degenerate input ranges (start >= end) to insert/remove are no-ops — callers feed us zero-length chunks all the time and it must be safe. Everything is u64 up to u64::MAX; the tests poke ranges right at the top of the domain, so no sneaky +1 arithmetic that wraps.

Acceptance: cargo test --offline. The tests pin exact span layouts after merge/split sequences (including a 14-step scripted session with the normalization invariant asserted after every step), the adjacency-merges/gap-of-one-does-not rule, covers vacuity, and gap windows clipped to both edges.
